articles.title: Hocuspocus 4: Add Real-Time Collaboration to Any App
articles.description: A plug-and-play real-time collaboration backend based on Yjs so you can quickly and safely wire up multi-user collaborative experiences into a JavaScript app. It runs on Node, Bun, Deno, or Cloudflare Workers. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Tiptap

articles.title: How to Evaluate an npm Package: 2026 Edition
articles.description: A practical checklist for vetting packages beyond star counts, covering provenance attestation, install scripts, CI quality, and maintainer responsiveness. Learn to spot the red flags before you npm install.
articles.author: Gabor Koos

articles.title: 🤖 Using AI to Write Better Code More Slowly
articles.description: A prolific JavaScript developer says LLMs aren’t just for pumping out bad code quickly, they can indeed help you write higher quality code more slowly.
articles.author: Nolan Lawson

articles.title: TypeScript Tips Everyone Should Know
articles.description: A concise set of tips for safer and cleaner code, as well as a reminder that while TypeScript can improve correctness, it doesn't guarantee good architecture or eliminate runtime bugs.
articles.author: Matt Smith

articles.title: Intentionally Blocking Rendering with JavaScript
articles.description: "Sometimes an inline render-blocking script is a small price to pay for avoiding aggressive layout shifts."
articles.author: Jay Freestone

articles.title: Why Does tsgo Use So Much Memory?
articles.description: A look into the Go-powered TypeScript 7 compiler and why it can chew through gigabytes on a large project.
articles.author: Zack Radisic

articles.title: 📊 Plotly 3.6: The Declarative Graphing Library
articles.description: A long-standing library, also widely used in the Python and R ecosystems, that offers over 50 visualization types, from basic charts and graphs to maps, plots, and heatmaps.
articles.author: Plotly, Inc.

articles.title: Component Party: A Rosetta Stone of UI Libraries
articles.description: A side-by-side code snippet comparison of frameworks including React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Ember, and more obscure options. Recent updates have extended Angular and Svelte coverage, plus added Ripple and Ember Polaris to the mix.
articles.author: Mathieu Schimmerling

articles.title: 🎉 tsParticles 4: A Particle Engine for Web Effects
articles.description: If you want a confetti cannon, fireworks, ribbons, fireflies, snow, or similar effects on your pages, this is for you. Supports all major frameworks and vanilla JS. You can see some live demos here.
articles.author: Matteo Bruni

articles.description: 🕒 The popular date-fns date utility library is preparing for a Temporal shaped future and becoming a "Temporal-first library". For now, a much leaner v4.4 is available, and v5.0 just entered alpha.
🔒 In the latest surfacing of Shai-Hulud, dozens of Red Hat npm packages have been backdoored.
npm 11.16.0 has shipped with initial (advisory warning only) support for an opt-in install-script policy using allowScripts. More details here.
The Svelte team presents its latest monthly update.
Ember 7.0 – A major release for the long-standing framework to remove deprecated features. This post rounds up everything new since 6.0.
Node.js 26.3.0 (Current) – npm is upgraded to 11.16.0, macOS x64 gets demoted to a tier 2 platform, and QUIC gets a big batch of improvements.
Astro 6.4 – The web framework adds a pluggable Markdown pipeline, a Rust-based Markdown processor, and helpers for advanced routing on Cloudflare.
pnpm 11.5.0/11.5.1, ESLint 10.4.1, Angular 22 RC3

articles.title: Accessible (I Think) Split-Cell Table Headers
articles.description: Eric Meyer tackles a niche <table> layout puzzle: diagonally-split table header cells like those in vintage NASA papers. He leans on rowspan and absolute positioning, while keeping things accessible with input from Alice Boxhall and Adrian Roselli.
articles.author: Eric Meyer

articles.title: 🤖 Chrome 149's Big Upgrades to AI Dev Assistance
articles.description: The recently announced MCP server and CLI for Chrome DevTools for agents is now officially stable in Chrome 149. It lets AI coding tools programmatically drive debugging, carry out perf audits, and more, should you want them to.
articles.author: Matthias Rohmer (Chrome for Developers)

articles.title: Introducing the HTML-in-Canvas API Origin Trial in Chrome
articles.description: HTML-in-Canvas lets you draw live, interactive HTML and CSS into a <canvas> element, combining DOM accessibility and text layout with performant low-level graphics. Handy for visualizations that need to stay fully interactable.
articles.author: Nattestad & Markoborodova (Chrome for Developers)

articles.title: Revealing Text with CSS letter-spacing
articles.description: Preethi shares a desire for something like ::nth-letter as a way to unlock creative, individual targeting of characters — but, until we have such a tool, she looks at what we can do today with letter-spacing, ::first-letter, and ::first-line.
articles.author: Preethi Sam

articles.title: Intentionally Blocking Rendering with JavaScript
articles.description: We’re usually told to keep scripts non-blocking with async/defer, but sometimes it’s worth holding back paint: blocking="render" keeps the parser running while preventing the layout flash when a component needs JavaScript to measure before it's shown.
articles.author: Jay Freestone

articles.title: Why the accept Attribute Degrades File Upload UX
articles.description: accept lets inputs specify which file types they accept (e.g. image/jpeg) but the UX is poor. Adam's recommendation is to ditch it and show a clearer error after submission.
articles.author: Adam Silver

articles.title: 📊 Frontend’s Missing Metric: The TBT Window
articles.description: Harry makes the case for tracking the "TBT Window", the FCP-to-TTI interval during which Total Blocking Time is counted. Since it shifts as FCP or TTI move, TBT can regress with no real change in blocking work.
articles.author: Harry Roberts

articles.title: ▶  Stop Using :invalid and :valid Pseudo-Classes: Use This Instead
articles.description: A ~3 minute video tip showcasing a better way to handle inline form validation using CSS, rather than resorting to JS or hacks.
articles.author: Zoran Jambor

articles.title: ▶  AI, Web and Standards
articles.description: A 12-minute talk on web features that make AI integration easier, and the standards needed to curb AI’s damage to the web.
articles.author: Hidde de Vries

articles.title: PolyCSS: Render 3D Meshes in the DOM with CSS
articles.description: Renders textured 3D in the DOM using CSS matrix3d() transforms with no WebGL or <canvas> needed. Each polygon is an element you can style and interact with. There’s a gallery to explore and a live builder to try. GitHub repo.
articles.author: LayoutitStudio

articles.title: Curlwind: A No-Build Tailwind Utility Generator
articles.description: Drop the CDN file into your project with the desired query params, which define only the classes you need, any variants, custom prefixes, and more.
articles.author: Steve Bauman

articles.title: Fontastic Space: Find Mathematically Optimal Font Pairings
articles.description: A free, browser-based tool to help you find a font pairing that’s mathematically optimal. It pairs based on multiple categories, including cap-height, ascender differences, and more.
articles.author: Dasha Dzisko

articles.title: 🤖 Code Wiki: Gemini-Generated Repo Documentation That’s Always Up-to-Date
articles.description: Designed to help developers quickly get up to speed on any new codebase by generating natural language summaries and architecture diagrams for any repository. Here’s an example using React’s repo.
articles.author: Google for Developers

articles.title: Markdown Printer: Chrome Extension to Save Pages as Markdown
articles.description: Potentially useful for feeding info to LLMs in AI development, converting files for use in documentation, or just saving files as notes.
articles.author: Lev Gelfenbuim

articles.title: All UtilityCSS: A Directory of Tailwind Templates, Components and Tools
articles.description: Includes both free and premium listings and you can filter by component category, resource type, tech stack, and the UI kit the resources are based on.
articles.author: Ajay & Anand Patel
